title: Repro Tab Workspace
concept: A friendly support-engineer workspace that treats each Playground as a top-level repro tab, then keeps Site, Files, Blueprint, Database, Logs, and Runtime as within-project tabs with all creation, saving, gallery, import, and export flows handled by inline panels.
focus: top-level project tabs for multiple Playgrounds
within-project tabs for Site Manager surfaces
beginner-friendly labels with restrained color blocks
flows: Switch between unsaved, saved, and PR preview Playgrounds as project tabs
Use within-project tabs for the active WordPress site, file browser, Blueprint editor, database tools, logs, and runtime settings
Start a Playground from vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, zip import, featured Blueprint shortcuts, or the full Blueprint gallery
Save temporary Playgrounds to browser storage or a local directory while seeing file-copy progress
Manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds with open, save, rename, and delete actions
Navigate the embedded WordPress site by path, refresh it, and jump to Homepage or WP Admin
Import from GitHub, run Blueprint URLs, import zip bundles, export to GitHub, download zip bundles, and download database or Blueprint artifacts
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
